name: Crab Cakes with Cilantro Butter
recipeIngredient: Bread crumbs, coarse ground homemade, 1 1/2 cups
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat 1/4 cup olive oil in a large skillet; cook red and green peppers until tender, about 3 to 4 minutes. Remove with a slotted spoon and cool.
2. Mix crab meat, eggs, cooked peppers, and 2 tablespoons bread crumbs in a large bowl. Season with salt and pepper. Shape into 8 equal-size cakes and place on a wax paper-lined plate. Chill for several hours.
3. Preheat oven to 375 degrees. Coat crab cakes with bread crumbs on all sides. Heat remaining 1/4 cup oil in an ovenproof skillet. Add crab cakes in a single layer and cook until browned on the bottom, about 2 minutes. Carefully turn cakes over and place in the oven. Bake until golden on all sides, 10 to 12 minutes.
4. For sauce: Boil white wine with shallot in a medium non-reactive saucepan until reduced to 1/3 cup. Stir in cream and boil until reduced by about half. Whisk in butter, 1 tablespoon at a time, over low heat until the sauce is light and thick. Do not allow it to boil or separate. Strain through a fine wire mesh strainer into a warm bowl. Stir in cilantro, salt, and pepper.
5. Serve by pouring a few tablespoons of sauce onto a warm plate. Place a crab cake on top and garnish with chopped tomato and cilantro sprig. Serve immediately.
